task: D5.1 GDN recurrent decode kernel audit
summary: Audit found no immediate vec8/thread/occupancy retune for qwen35_gdn_recurrent_rmsnorm_gate_lowp_kernel on c=1 decode. The hot Qwen3.5 shape already uses 8-way unrolled head-k loops, 128 threads for 128 value lanes, VGPR=56, and Scratch_Size=0. No runtime/kernel default changed; keep the existing kernel and close D5.1 with a stop condition.

shape.note: rocprof reports Grid_Size_X as total work-items (32 blocks * 128 threads = 4096). LDS_Block_Size is reported as 0 for this dynamic-shared launch, so the wrapper-computed dynamic shared byte count is recorded separately.

summary.kernel_name: void (anonymous namespace)::qwen35_gdn_recurrent_rmsnorm_gate_lowp_kernel<unsigned short>(float const*, unsigned short const*, unsigned short const*, unsigned short const*, float const*, float const*, float const*, float*, float*, float, long, long, long, long)

void (anonymous namespace)::qwen35_gdn_recurrent_rmsnorm_gate_lowp_kernel<_Float16>(float const*, _Float16 const*, _Float16 const*, _Float16 const*, float const*, float const*, float const*, float*, float*, float, long, long, long, long)

current_structure: Q/K RMS load loop is already 8-way unrolled over head_k_dim: for idx = threadIdx.x * 8; idx += blockDim.x * 8.
KV memory accumulation and recurrent-state update loops are already 8-way unrolled over head_k_dim.
For Qwen3.5 c=1 decode, head_v_dim is 128 and the kernel maps value_idx = threadIdx.x, so 128 threads exactly cover one value lane per thread.
The C ABI wrapper fixes threads=128 and computes dynamic shared as (2 * head_k_dim + 3 * threads + head_v_dim) * sizeof(float) = 3072 bytes for the Qwen3.5 shape.

The kernel uses shared q/k staging and reductions with barriers. Removing barriers is explicitly disallowed without a new correctness proof because prior GDN barrier-removal attempts corrupted recurrent state.
headroom_assessment: A 64-thread retune would not cover all 128 output value lanes without a new multi-value-per-thread ownership scheme and changed reductions.
A 256-thread retune would add idle value lanes and larger reductions without new work; D5.2 showed the same reduction-overhead pattern for larger workgroups on small decode kernels.

Vectorizing across value lanes would require strided recurrent-state writes/reads and multiple value accumulators per thread, raising VGPR pressure; the local vec8 target over head_k_dim is already present.
VGPR=56 and Scratch_Size=0 are below ROOFLINE occupancy concern thresholds (<=96 VGPR is in the max-occupancy band; scratch on hot decode would be a bug).

rationale: M.4 makes GDN decode visible but bounded: 30 calls/token and 5.23%/5.39%/4.84% of decode kernel time at 512/4K/32K.
The current kernel already implements the requested local vec8 head-k unroll; the remaining per-value recurrence is sequential state math, not a simple vector-width miss.
Resource metadata is clean on the representative Qwen3.5 c=1 shape: VGPR=56, Scratch_Size=0, 128-thread workgroups, and ~8.7 us median dispatch time for both BF16 and FP16 lowp variants.
Thread-count retuning is structurally unsafe/no-op without a new kernel: 128 threads exactly match head_v_dim=128; smaller workgroups miss lanes and larger ones add idle reduction participants.
Any deeper change (multi-value per thread, barrier removal, adjacent fusion) crosses into kernel R&D and needs parent-workspace evidence plus a stronger oracle before porting.
decision.future_reopen_condition: Reopen only if parent kernel R&D produces a correct multi-value/thread or barrier-reduced GDN design with rocprof resource metadata and E2E decode improvement, or if M.4-style profiling shows GDN decode growing into a multi-percent larger bucket after other lanes change.

commands:
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
from hipengine.kernels.hip_gfx1100.linear_attn import build_qwen35_linear_attn_gdn
cv = Path('/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt').read_text()
build_qwen35_linear_attn_gdn(load=True, compiler_version=cv, require_cached=False)
print('gdn build OK')
PY
